assessment: The SKIP filter is working as intended this week. Taken trades won at 83.3% (10/12) while shadow trades won at only 55.0% (33/60), a 28-percentage-point gap that indicates the agent is successfully identifying higher-quality opportunities and correctly discarding lower-quality ones. The 55% shadow win rate is only marginally above the ~50% baseline one would expect from a coin-flip market, confirming that skipped games carried little exploitable edge. The filter is adding clear value.
sample_size_note: The taken-trade bucket has only 12 resolved outcomes, well below the 30-outcome threshold for reliable inference, so no threshold changes are warranted this week — the strong win-rate differential is encouraging but too noisy to act on without risking overfitting.
